name: Authentication and User Management
description: Secure your MongoDB deployment by enabling authentication, creating users with specific roles, and managing access control.

steps.title: Create an admin user
steps.description: Create a superuser in the admin database before enabling authentication.
steps.command: mongosh --eval 'db.getSiblingDB("admin").createUser({user: "admin", pwd: "secure_admin_pass", roles: [{role: "userAdminAnyDatabase", db: "admin"}, {role: "readWriteAnyDatabase", db: "admin"}, {role: "clusterAdmin", db: "admin"}]})'
steps.warning: Create the admin user BEFORE enabling authentication. Once auth is enabled, you cannot connect without credentials.

steps.title: Create an application user with specific database access
steps.description: Add a user with read-write access limited to a single database.
steps.command: mongosh -u admin -p secure_admin_pass --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.getSiblingDB("mydb").createUser({user: "app_user", pwd: "app_secure_pass", roles: [{role: "readWrite", db: "mydb"}]})'

steps.title: Create a read-only user
steps.description: Add a user that can only read data for reporting or analytics.
steps.command: mongosh -u admin -p secure_admin_pass --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.getSiblingDB("mydb").createUser({user: "readonly_user", pwd: "readonly_pass", roles: [{role: "read", db: "mydb"}]})'

steps.title: Enable authentication in mongod config
steps.description: Update the MongoDB configuration to require authentication for all connections.
steps.command: echo -e 'security:\n  authorization: enabled' | sudo tee -a /etc/mongod.conf && sudo systemctl restart mongod
steps.tip: After this restart, all connections must authenticate. Test with: mongosh -u admin -p secure_admin_pass --authenticationDatabase admin

steps.title: List all users in a database
steps.description: View existing users and their assigned roles.

faq.question: What built-in roles are available?
faq.answer: Common roles: read (read-only), readWrite (read and write), dbAdmin (schema and index management), userAdmin (user management), clusterAdmin (replica set and sharding admin), root (superuser). You can also create custom roles with fine-grained privileges.

faq.question: How do I change a user password?
faq.answer: Connect as admin and run: db.getSiblingDB('mydb').changeUserPassword('app_user', 'new_secure_password'). The change takes effect immediately for new connections.
